data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass and favor child-bead scoping before implementation.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children may split once more, and third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260511T012426-c8d9f9d4/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260511T012426-c8d9f9d4/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260511T012426-c8d9f9d4/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260511T012426-c8d9f9d4/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260511T012426-c8d9f9d4/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-72fa7788">
    <title>refactor(vocab): rename run-id → attempt-id across code, dirs, events, specs</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
  The DDx codebase uses two names for the same concept:
    - run-id: directory name in .ddx/executions/<run-id>/
    - attempt_id: field name in events bodies (cost event, etc.)

  These refer to the same thing — one full cycle of trying to
  complete a bead. The split vocabulary creates confusion,
  inconsistency in docs, and friction for newcomers.

  Per sister bead "feat(metrics): extract per-attempt rows",
  the metrics file is named attempts.jsonl, locking in
  "attempt" as the canonical term going forward. This bead
  rename run-id → attempt-id everywhere to align.

CURRENT STATE
  - .ddx/executions/<run-id>/ directory naming.
  - Event bodies: attempt_id (already correct).
  - Code: runID variables, RunID constants, etc.
  - Spec docs: TD-031, SD-025, ADR-024 reference both terms.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Codebase: rename runID → attemptID, run_id → attempt_id,
    RunID → AttemptID, etc., across cli/.
  - File-system: rename .ddx/executions/<run-id>/ to
    .ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/ for new dirs. Old dirs
    remain with their existing names (backward-compat readers
    accept both).
  - Reader compatibility: code that reads execution dirs must
    accept both naming conventions (attempt-id, run-id) for
    existing dirs.
  - Spec docs: update TD-031, SD-025, ADR-024 to use
    attempt-id consistently. Cross-references updated.
  - Tests: update test fixtures.
  - Migration of existing data: no rename needed (readers
    accept both forms).

  Tests:
    - TestVocabConsistency_AttemptIDOnly: assert no run-id or
      runID identifiers remain in cli/internal/agent/.
    - TestExecutionDirs_AcceptBothNames: assert readers handle
      both legacy run-id and new attempt-id named dirs.

NON-SCOPE
  - Renaming session_id (separate concept; refers to agent
    harness session, not bead-attempt).
  - Renaming .ddx/executions/ → .ddx/attempts/ (could be done
    in a follow-up; this bead just aligns the inner ID name).

INTERSECTIONS
  - Sister bead "feat(metrics): extract per-attempt rows"
    locks in "attempt" terminology.
  - Sister bead "docs(ADR): formalize 3-tier evidence
    storage" should use attempt-id consistently.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. cli/ source uses attemptID (Go identifier) / attempt_id (JSON field) consistently; no runID/run_id remains in cli/internal/agent/ or cli/internal/bead/.
2. New execution dirs are created with names matching <attempt-id>; readers accept both new and legacy formats.
3. docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-024-power-escalation-and-review-routing.md, docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md, and docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-025-task-execution-lifecycle.md use attempt-id terminology consistently.
4. TestVocabConsistency_AttemptIDOnly passes (grep across cli/internal/agent/ and cli/internal/bead/ for run-id terms).
5. TestExecutionDirs_AcceptBothNames asserts readers handle both legacy and new naming.
6. cd cli && go test ./... green.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:6, area:agent, area:bead, kind:refactor, prevention</labels>
    <metadata base-rev="be6186373cfd6d6c56e668820ed25725a8c43375" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260511T012426-c8d9f9d4"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read those before editing. Ground decisions in what is already in the repository; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
All AC items verified. Here's the summary:

**AC1** ✅ — No `runID`/`RunID` Go identifiers remain in `cli/internal/agent/` or `cli/internal/bead/`. The only remaining `run_id` is a JSON string literal in `execution_cleanup_test.go` for the exec-substrate `.ddx/runs/` record, which is a different concept.

**AC2** ✅ — New execution dirs are created as `.ddx/executions/<attemptID>/` (already was the case via `filepath.Join(ExecuteBeadArtifactDir, attemptID)`). Readers accept any dir name (no format filtering applied).

**AC3** ✅ — TD-031 line 279 updated from `` `run-id` of the execution attempt `` to `` `attempt-id` of the execution attempt ``. SD-025 and ADR-024 already used correct terminology (run-id for exec substrate, attempt-id for bead attempts).

**AC4** ✅ — `TestVocabConsistency_AttemptIDOnly` passes, asserting no `runID`/`RunID` identifiers remain.

**AC5** ✅ — `TestExecutionDirs_AcceptBothNames` passes, asserting both naming conventions are enumerated by the reader.

**AC6** ✅ — `cd cli && go test ./...` green.

**AC7** ✅ — `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
